name: Sushi
recipeInstructions:
1. Wash rice in a large bowl of water. Rub grains gently; wet grains break easily.
2. Drain water. Repeat this step until water is almost clear.
3. Let rice sit for at least 30 minutes in summer and 1 hour in winter.
4. Mix rice with correct amount of water in a cooking pot. Cover with lid.
5. Cook rice over medium heat until water boils.
6. When it begins to boil, turn heat to high and cook for 1 minute.
7. Turn heat to low and cook for 4 or 5 minutes.
8. Reduce heat to the lowest setting for 10 minutes. Every grain of rice absorbs water and becomes plump.
9. Turn off heat and let rice stand, covered with lid for 10 minutes.
Mix vinegar, salt, and sugar in a cup and heat until dissolved. (Use microwave)
Put rice in a large glass, wooden, or plastic bowl (not metal). Spread rice evenly over the bottom. Sprinkle vinegar mixture generously over the rice.
Building the Sushi:
1. Spread plastic wrap on bamboo sushi mat.
2. Lay roasted seaweed sheet on plastic wrap.
3. Spread sushi rice on seaweed (about 1 cup), flatten to ¼ inch with back of spoon.
4. Lay a strip of veggies along one end.
5. Spread a teaspoon of mayo mustard mixture.
6. Roll up carefully, press the end. Remove mat and unwrap the plastic.
7. Repeat using all ingredients to make 4 rolls. Cut each roll crosswise into 6 to 8 equal slices.
Serve with wasabi, soy sauce, Braggs Amino, or any kind of mustard. I like it plain! This makes a super plate of appetizers or a complete meal for two, along with a salad or whatever! It keeps well for a day or two and is delicious cold the next day.
